9421.text: (2) the complexity (or otherwise) of the case;

9422.text: (3) any respects in which, in connection with the Company’s affairs, there falls on the provisional liquidator any responsibility of an exceptional kind or degree;

9423.text: (4) the effectiveness with which the provisional liquidator appears to be carrying out, or to have carried out, his duties; and

9424.text: (5) the value and nature of the property with which he has had to deal.

9426.text: Without prejudice to any order the Court may make as to costs, the provisional liquidator’s remuneration shall be paid to him, and the amount of any expenses incurred by him reimbursed:

9427.text: (1) if a winding-up order is not made, out of the property of the Company; and

9428.text: (2) if a winding-up order is made, as an expense of the liquidation, in the prescribed order of priority.

9430.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs, in a case falling within Rule 54.113(1) above the provisional liquidator may retain out of the company’s property such sums or property as are or may be required for meeting his remuneration and expenses.

9433.text: The appointment of the provisional liquidator shall be terminated without further order on the appointment of a liquidator and may be terminated by the Court on his application or on that of any of the persons specified in Rule 54.99.

9435.text: If the provisional liquidator’s appointment terminates, whether in consequence of the dismissal of the winding-up petition or otherwise, the Court may give such directions as it thinks fit with respect to the accounts of his administration or any other matters which it thinks appropriate.

9437.text: Notice of termination of the appointment of a provisional liquidator must be given by the provisional liquidator, unless the termination is on the making of a winding-up order or the Court otherwise directs. Such notice must as soon as reasonably practicable be:

9440.text: In addition to the standard contents (as set out in Section VII), a notice under Rule 54.117 must state:

9442.text: V APPLICATION FOR A CELL RECEIVERSHIP ORDER

9445.text: This Section applies in relation to the making of a cell receivership order under Regulation 8.2.

9446.text: Application For A Cell Receivership Order

9448.text: An application for a cell receivership application shall be in such form as the Court may specify.

9450.text: If made by the Company or by its directors, an application for a cell receivership order shall state the name of the Company and its address for service, which (in the absence of special reasons to the contrary) is that of the Company’s registered office.

9452.text: If the application is made by the directors, it shall state that it is made under Regulation 8.3.1(b); but from and after making it is to be treated for all purposes as the application of the Company.

9454.text: If made by any other person (including the DFSA ), an application for a cell receivership order shall state the name and address for service of the applicant.

9456.text: The application shall contain a statement of the applicant’s belief that the cellular assets attributable to a particular cell of the Company are or are likely to be insufficient to discharge the claims of creditors in respect of that cell.

9459.text: There shall be attached to the application a written statement which shall be in such form as the Court may specify by each of the persons proposed to be a cell receiver stating:

9460.text: (1) that he consents to accept appointment;

9461.text: (2) details of any prior professional relationship(s) that he has had with the applicant or the Company to which he is to be appointed as cell receiver; and

9462.text: (3) his opinion that it is reasonably likely that one or more of the purposes set out in Regulation 8.2.3 would be achieved.

9463.text: Witness Statement In Support Of Cell Receivership Application

9465.text: Where it is proposed to apply to the Court for a cell receivership order to be made in relation to a Company, a witness statement complying with Rules 54.127 to 54.129 must be prepared, with a view to its being filed at Court in support of the application.

9467.text: If the application is to be made by the Company or by its directors, the witness statement shall be made by one of the directors, or the secretary of the Company, stating himself to make it on behalf of the company or, as the case may be, on behalf of the directors.

9469.text: If the application is to be made by any other person, the witness statement shall be made by the applicant or a person acting under the authority of the applicant, and there must be stated in the witness statement his means of knowledge of the matters to which the witness statement relates and the nature of his authority (if applicable).

9471.text: The witness statement in support of an application for a cell receivership order shall contain:

9472.text: (1) a statement of the financial position in respect of that cell, specifying (to the best of the applicant’s knowledge and belief) the assets and liabilities of the cell, including contingent and prospective liabilities;

9473.text: (2) details of any security known or believed to be held by creditors in respect of that cell;

9474.text: (3) details of any debt or liability owing to the applicant by that cell and any security held by the applicant in respect of that cell;

9475.text: (4) details of the nature of the business of that cell and, if different, the business of the Company as a whole;

9476.text: (5) details of any insolvency proceedings in relation to that cell or to the Company, including any

9477.text: petition that has been presented for the winding up of the Company or any administrative receiver who has been appointed in respect of the Company so far as within the immediate knowledge of the applicant; and

9478.text: (6) any other matters which, in the opinion of those intending to make the application for a cell receivership order, will assist the Court in deciding whether to make such an order, so far as lying within the knowledge or belief of the witness.

9481.text: The application (and all supporting documents) shall be filed with the Court , with a sufficient number of copies for service and use as provided by Rule 54.134.

9483.text: Each of the copies filed shall have applied to it the seal of the Court and be issued to the applicant.

9485.text: The Court shall fix a venue for the hearing of the application and this shall be endorsed on each copy of the application issued under Rule 54.131.

9487.text: After the application is filed, it is the duty of the applicant to notify the Court in writing of the existence of any insolvency proceedings in relation to the Company as soon as he becomes aware of them.

9490.text: The application shall be served in addition to those persons referred to in Regulation 8.3.3:

9491.text: (1) if there is pending a petition for the winding-up of the Company, on the petitioner (and also on the provisional liquidator) if any;

9492.text: (2) on the person proposed as the cell receiver; and

9493.text: (3) if a supervisor of a voluntary arrangement under Part 2 of the Law has been appointed, on him.

9494.text: In this Rule, references to the application are to a copy of the application issued by the Court under Rule 54.131 together with the witness statement required by Rule 54.126 and the documents attached to the application.

9495.text: Notice To Officers Charged With Execution Of Legal Process, Etc.

9497.text: The applicant shall as soon as reasonably practicable after filing the application give notice of its being

9499.text: (1) any enforcement officer or other officer who to his knowledge is charged with an execution or other legal process either against that cell of the Company or its property or against the Company or its property generally; and

9500.text: (2) any person who to his knowledge has distrained either against that cell of the Company or its property or against the Company or its property generally.

9501.text: Manner In Which Service Is To Be Effected

9503.text: Service of the application in accordance with 54.134 shall be effected by the applicant, or his legal practitioner, or by a person instructed by him or his legal practitioner, not less than five (5) business days before the date fixed for the hearing.

9505.text: Service shall be effected as follows:

9506.text:
(1) on the Company (subject to Rule 54.138 below), by delivering the documents to its registered office; (2) on any other person (subject to Rule 54.139 below), by delivering the documents to his proper address; or   
(3) in either case, in such other manner as the Court may direct.

9508.text: If delivery to a Company’s registered office is not practicable, service may be effected by delivery to its last known principal place of business.

9510.text: For the purposes of Rule 54.137 (2) above, a person’s proper address is any which he has previously notified as his address for service; but if he has not notified any such address, service may be effected by delivery to his usual or last known address.

9513.text: Service of the application must be verified by a certificate of service. The certificate of service must be sufficient to identify the application served and must specify:

9516.text: The certificate of service shall be filed with the Court as soon as reasonably practicable after service, and in any event not less than one (1) business day before the hearing of the application.

9517.text: Application Where Company In Liquidation Or Administrative Receivership

9519.text: Where an application is made for a cell receivership order under Regulation 8.2.4, the witness statement required by Rule 54.126 shall contain:

9520.text: (1) full details of the existing insolvency proceedings, including the name and address of the liquidator or administrative receiver, the date he was appointed and by whom;

9521.text: (2) the reasons why it has subsequently been considered appropriate that an application for a cell receivership order should be made;

9522.text: (3) all other matters that would, in the opinion of the applicant, assist the Court in considered the need to make provisions in respect of matters arising in connection with the liquidation or administrative receivership; and

9526.text: At the hearing of an application for a cell receivership order, any of the following persons may appear or be represented:

9529.text: If the Court makes a cell receivership order, it shall be in such form as the Court may specify.

9531.text: If the Court makes a cell receivership order, the costs of the applicant and of any person whose costs are allowed by the Court , are payable as an expense of the cell receivership.

9533.text: Where the Court makes a cell receivership order in relation to a Company upon an application under Regulation 8.2.4, the Court shall include in the order (i) such provision as the Court thinks fit with respect to matters arising in connection with the liquidation or administrative receivership and (ii) such other provisions as the Court shall think fit.

9536.text: If the Court makes a cell receivership order, it shall as soon as reasonably practicable send two sealed copies of the order to the person who made the application.

9538.text: The applicant shall send a sealed copy of the order as soon as reasonably practicable to:

9539.text: (1) the person appointed as cell receiver; and (2) if the Company is an Authorised Person, the DFSA .

9541.text: If the Court makes an order under Regulation 8.3.2, it shall give directions as to the persons to whom, and how, notice of that order is to be given.

9545.text: Subject to Rule 54.151, this Section applies in relation to the service of:

9547.text: which are required to be served by any provision of the Law, the Regulations or this Part of the Rules (“court documents”).

9549.text: This Section does not apply to the service of:

9550.text:
(1) a winding up petition;   
(2) any document relating to such a petition; or (3) a winding-up order.

9553.text: Except where different provision is made in this Part and subject to any direction or order of the Court , Part 9 (Service ) applies in relation to the service of court documents, whether within or outside the jurisdiction.

9555.text: For the purpose of the application by Rule 54.152 of Part 9 (Service ) to the service of documents in insolvency proceedings an application commencing insolvency proceedings or an application within insolvency proceedings against a respondent is to be treated as a claim form.

9558.text: Where in insolvency proceedings the Court makes an order staying any action, execution or other legal process against the property of a Company, service of the order may be effected by sending a sealed copy of the order to whatever is the address for service of the party having the carriage of the proceedings to be stayed.

9561.text: Where there are joint office-holders in insolvency proceedings, service on one of them is to be treated as service on all of them.

9562.text: VII CONTENT OF NOTICES TO BE ADVERTISED

9564.text: Where under the Law, the Regulations or this Part of the Rules a notice is to be advertised, in addition to any content specifically required by the Law, the Regulations or any other provision of this Part of the Rules , the content of such a notice must be as set out in this Section.

9566.text: All notices published must specify insofar as it is applicable in relation to the particular notice:

9567.text: (1) the name and postal address of the office-holder acting in the proceedings;

9568.text: (2) the capacity in which the office-holder is acting and the date of appointment;

9569.text: (3) either an e-mail address, or a telephone number, through which the office-holder may be contacted;

9570.text: (4) the name of any person other than the office-holder (if any) who may be contacted regarding the proceedings;

9571.text: (5) the Court name and any number assigned to the proceedings by the Court ;

9572.text: (6) the registered name and number of the Company;

9573.text: (7) the registered office of the Company or, in the case of a Recognised Company, its place of business in the DIFC or, in the case of any other Foreign Company, its registered office and, if different, its principal place of business;

9574.text: (8) any principal trading address if this is different from its registered office;

9575.text: (9) any name under which it was registered in the 12 months prior to the date of the commencement of the proceedings which are the subject of the notice; and

9576.text: (10) any name or style (other than its registered name) under which:

9577.text: (a) the Company carried on business; or

9578.text: (b) any debt owed to a creditor was incurred.

9580.text: Information required under this Section to be included in a notice to be advertised may be omitted if it is not reasonably practicable to obtain it.

9584.text: This Section applies in relation to costs in connection with insolvency proceedings.

9586.text: In this Section a reference to costs includes charges and expenses.

9587.text: Requirement To Assess Costs By The Detailed Procedure

9589.text: Where the costs of any person are payable as an expense out of the insolvent estate in accordance with the Law, the Regulations or by order of the Court , the amount payable must be decided by detailed assessment unless agreed between the office-holder and the person entitled to payment.

9591.text: In the absence of such agreement as is mentioned in Rule 54.161, the office-holder:

9592.text: (1) may serve notice requiring that person to commence detailed assessment proceedings in accordance with Part 40 (Procedure for Detailed Assessment of Costs); and

9593.text: (2) must serve such notice (except in an administrative receivership) where a liquidation or creditors’ committee formed in relation to the insolvency proceedings resolves that the amount of the costs must be decided by detailed assessment.

9595.text: Where the costs of any person employed by an office-holder in insolvency proceedings are required to be decided by detailed assessment under an order of the Court directing that those costs are to be paid otherwise than out of the insolvent estate, the Registrar shall note on the final costs certificate by whom, or the manner in which, the costs are to be paid.

9597.text: Where an office-holder is made a party to any proceedings on the application of another party to the proceedings, he shall not be personally be liable for costs unless the Court otherwise directs.

9599.text: Nothing in Rules 54.161 to 54.164 above shall affect the power of the Court to order an immediate assessment of the costs of any proceedings, or part of any proceedings, before the Court pursuant to Part 38 (General Rules About Costs).

9602.text: This Section applies where a party to, or person affected by, any proceedings in an insolvency:

9603.text: (1) applies to the Court for an order allowing his costs, or part of them, incidental to the proceedings; and

9604.text: (2) that application is not made at the time of the proceedings.

9606.text: The person concerned shall serve a sealed copy of his or her application on the office-holder.

9608.text: The office-holder may appear on an application to which Rule 54.166 applies.

9610.text: No costs of or incidental to the application shall be allowed to the applicant unless the Court is satisfied that

9611.text: the application could not have been made at the time of the proceedings.

9614.text: Except as directed by the Court , no allowance as a witness in any examination or other proceedings before the Court shall be made to any officer of the insolvent Company to which the proceedings relate.

9616.text: A person presenting any petition in a Company insolvency shall not be regarded as a witness on the hearing of the petition, but the Registrar may allow his expenses of travelling and subsistence.

9619.text: A final costs certificate of the Registrar is final and conclusive as to all matters which have not been objected to in the manner provided for under these Rules .

9621.text: Where it is proved to the satisfaction of the Registrar that a final costs certificate has been lost or destroyed, he may issue a duplicate.

9623.text: Appeals And Reviews Of Court Orders

9625.text: The Court may review, rescind, or vary any order made by it in the exercise of its jurisdiction under this Part.

9627.text: Appeals in proceedings under this Part lie as follows:

9628.text: (1) to a Judge of the Court of First Instance where the decision appealed against is made by the Registrar ; and

9629.text: (2) to the Court of Appeal from a decision of a Judge of the Court of First Instance.

9631.text: Any application for the rescission of a winding-up order shall be made within five (5) business days after the date on which the order was made.

9634.text: An appeal against a decision at first instance may only be brought with either the permission of the Court which made the decision or the permission of the Court which has jurisdiction to hear the appeal.

9636.text: An appellant must file an appeal notice (within the meaning of Part 44 (Appeals)) within 21 calendar days after the date of the decision of the Court that the appellant wishes to appeal.

9638.text: Subject to Rules 54.174 to 54.178, the procedure in Part 44 (Appeals) applies to any appeal to which this Section applies.

9639.text: X PRINCIPAL COURT RULES AND PRACTICE TO APPLY

9640.text: Principal Court Rules And Practice To Apply

9642.text: The provisions of the Rules in the first column of the table in this Rule (including any related practice directions) apply to insolvency proceedings by virtue of the provisions of the Rules in this Part set out in the second column with any necessary modifications, except so far as inconsistent with these Rules :

9643.text:
Provisions of the RulesProvisions of this Part
Part 19 (Further Information)Rule 54.182(1)
Part 28 (Production of Documents)Rule 54.182(2)
Part 29 (Evidence)Rule 54.16
Part 33 (Payments into Court)Rule 54.184
Part 40 (Procedure for Detailed Assessment of Costs)Rule 54.94(2) and 54.162(1)
Part 44 (Appeals)Section IX
Part 45 (General Rules About Enforcement of Judgments and Orders)Rule 54.30

9645.text: The provisions of the other Parts of these Rules (including any related practice directions) not referred to in the table in Rule 54.180 apply to proceedings under the Law, the Regulations and this Part of the Rules with any necessary modifications, except so far as inconsistent with the Rules contained in this Part.

9648.text: Any party to insolvency proceedings may apply to the Court for an order:

9650.text: (a) clarify any matter which is in dispute in the proceedings; or

9651.text: (b) give additional information in relation to any such matter,

9652.text: in accordance with Part 19 (Further Information); or

9653.text: (2) to obtain documents from any other party in accordance with Part 28 (Production of Documents).

9655.text: An application under Rule 54.182 may be made without notice being given to any other person.

9658.text: Part 33 (Payments into Court) applies to money lodged in Court under the Law, the Regulations or otherwise in relation to an insolvency proceeding under this Part.

9659.text: No Determination Of Insolvency Proceedings By The Small Claims Tribunal

9661.text: No insolvency proceedings may be issued in or be transferred to the Small Claims Tribunal under Part 53 (The Small Claims Tribunal) and Part 53 shall not apply to insolvency proceedings.

9664.text: Any person who has under the Law, Regulations of this Part of the Rules the right to inspect the Court file of insolvency proceedings may require the Court to provide him with an office copy of any document from the file.

9666.text: A person’s rights under Rule 54.186 may be exercised on his behalf by his legal representative.

9668.text: An office copy provided by the Court under Rule 54.186 shall be in such form as the Court may specify, and shall bear the Court ‘s seal.

9671.text: No insolvency proceedings shall be invalidated by any formal defect or by any irregularity, unless the Court before which objection is made considers that substantial injustice has been caused by the defect or irregularity, and that the injustice cannot be remedied by any order of the Court .

9675.text: This Section applies to applications to the Court for an order under Article 25(3) of the Law (Powers of liquidator) or Articles 94 to 96 of the Law (Inquiry into Company’s dealings).

9678.text: An application for such an order shall be in writing specifying the grounds on which it is made.

9680.text: The application must specify the name of the respondent.

9682.text: It shall be stated whether the application is for an order that the respondent:

9684.text: (2) submit witness statements (and, if so, the particulars to be given of the matters to be included);

9685.text: (3) produce books, papers or other records (if so, the items in question to be specified);

9686.text: (4) clarify any matter which is in dispute in the proceedings or give additional information in relation to any such matter and, if so, Part 19 (Further Information) shall apply to any such order; or

9687.text: or for any two or more of those purposes or for some other (and, if so, what other) relief.

9689.text: The application may be made without notice to any party other than the respondent.

9692.text: The Court may, whatever the purpose of the application, make any order which it has power to make under the Law and the Regulations.

9694.text: The Court , if it orders the respondent to appear before it, shall specify a date and time for his appearance, which shall be not less than fourteen (14) calendar days from the date of the order.

9696.text: If the respondent is ordered to submit a witness statement, the order shall specify:

9697.text: (1) the matters which are to be dealt with in his witness statement; and

9698.text: (2) the time within which the witness statement is to be submitted to the Court .

9700.text: If the order is to produce books, papers or other records, the time and manner of compliance shall be specified.

9702.text: The order must be served as soon as reasonably practicable on the respondent; and it must be served personally, unless the Court orders otherwise.

9705.text: At any examination of the respondent, the applicant may attend in person, or be represented by a legal representative, and may put such questions to the respondent as the Court may allow.

9707.text: The following persons may attend the examination with the permission of the Court and may put questions to the respondent (but only through the applicant):

9708.text: (1) any person who could have applied for an order under the applicable provisions of the Law;

9709.text: (2) any creditor who has provided information on which the application was made by the applicant.

9711.text: If the respondent is ordered to clarify any matter or to give additional information, the Court shall direct him as to the questions which he is required to answer, and as to whether his answers (if any) are to be made in a witness statement.

9713.text: The respondent may at his own expense instruct a legal representative, who may put to him such questions as the Court may allow for the purpose of enabling him to explain or qualify any answers given him, and may make representations on his behalf.

9716.text: There shall be made in writing such record of the examination as the Court thinks proper. The record shall be read over either to or by the respondent and signed or otherwise authenticated by him on a date and at a time fixed by the Court .

9718.text: The written record may, in any proceedings (whether under the Law or otherwise) be used as evidence against the respondent of any statement made by him in the course of his examination.

9719.text: Inspection Of Record Of Examination, Etc.

9721.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs, the written record of questions put to the respondent and the respondent’s answers, and any witness statements submitted by the respondent in compliance with an order of the Court under the applicable provision of the Law, are not to be filed with the Court .

9723.text: The documents set out in Rule 54.208 are not open to inspection without an order of the Court by any person other than:

9724.text: (1) the applicant for an order under the applicable provision of the Law; or

9725.text: (2) any person who could have applied for such an order in respect of the affairs of the same Company.

9727.text: The documents to which Rule 54.207 refers are:

9730.text: The Court may from time to time give directions as to the custody and inspection of any documents to which Rules 54.206 to 54.208 apply, and as to the furnishing of copies of, or extracts from, such documents.

9733.text: Where the Court makes any other order against a person (including, without limitation, an order requiring the production of books, papers or other records or clarifying any matter which is in dispute or giving additional information) the costs of the application for the order may be ordered by the Court to be paid by the respondent.

9735.text: Where the Court has ordered an examination of any person under the applicable provision of the Law, and it appears to it that the examination was made necessary because information had been unjustifiably refused by that person, it may order that the costs of the examination be paid by him.

9737.text: Subject to Rules 54.210 and 54.211, the applicant’s costs shall, unless the Court otherwise orders, be paid as an expense of the insolvency proceeding.

9739.text: The costs of a person summoned to attend for examination under this Part incurred in connection with his attendance are at the Court ‘s discretion.

9740.text: XII APPLICATION OF PART [X] TO LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIPS

9742.text: This Part shall apply for the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of the Law and the Regulations which are applied by Article 87 (Application to Limited Liability Partnerships) with the following modifications (except where the context otherwise requires):

9743.text: 54.214.1 references to a Company shall include references to a Limited Liability Partnership;

9744.text: 54.214.2 references to a director or to an officer of a Company shall include references to a member of Limited Liability Partnership;

9745.text: 54.214.3 references to the Law or the Regulations, or to any provisions thereof, shall include references to the Law or the Regulations, or the provisions thereof, as they apply to Limited Liability Partnerships in accordance with the Law;

9746.text: 54.214.4 such further or other modifications as the context may require for the purpose of giving effect to (i) the Law and the Regulations in accordance with Article 87 of the Law and (ii) this Rule 54.214.

9748.text: Forms For Use In Insolvency Proceedings

9750.text: Forms, as specified in this Part and where provided, are to be used in insolvency proceedings.

9752.text: Where the forms are required to be used, they may be used with such variations, if any, as the circumstances may require.

9756.text: The Court shall open and maintain a file in any case where documents are filed with it under the Law, the Regulations or this Part of the Rules .

9758.text: Any documents which are filed with the Court under the Law or the Regulations must be placed on the file opened in accordance with Rule 54.217.

9760.text: The following persons may inspect or obtain from the Court a copy of, or a copy of any document or documents contained in, a file opened in accordance with Rule 54.217:

9763.text: The right to inspect or obtain a copy of, or a copy of any document or documents contained in, the file opened in accordance with Rule 54.217 may be exercised on that person’s behalf by a person authorised to do so by that person.

9765.text: Any person who is not otherwise entitled to inspect or obtain a copy of, or a copy of any document or documents contained in, the file opened in accordance with Rule 54.217 may do so if that person has the permission of the Court .

9767.text: The Court may, upon an application by the office-holder or any person appearing to the Court to have an interest, direct that the file, a document (or part of it) or a copy of a document (or part of it) must not be made available under Rules 54.219 to 54.220 without the permission of the Court .

9769.text: Where any person wishes to exercise the right to inspect the file under Rule 54.219, 54.220 or 54.221, that person:

9770.text: (1) if the permission of the Court is required, must file with the Court an application notice in accordance with this Part; or

9771.text: (2) if the permission of the Court is not required, may inspect the file at any reasonable time.

9773.text: Where any person wishes to exercise the right to obtain a copy of a document under Rule 54.219, 54.220 or 54.221, that person must pay any prescribed fee and:

9774.text: (1) if the permission of the Court is required, file with the Court an application notice in accordance with this Part; or (2) if the permission of the Court is not required, file with the Court a written request for the document.

9776.text: An application for (a) permission to inspect the file or obtain a copy of a document under Rule 54.221 or (b) a direction under Rule 54.222 may be made without notice to any other party, but the Court may direct that notice shall be given to any person who would be affected by its decision.

9777.text: XV BLOCK TRANSFERS OF CASES WHERE INSOLVENCYPRACTITIONER HAS RETIRED ETC.

9780.text: The Rules in this Section relate to applications for a block transfer order.

9781.text: Power To Make A Block Transfer Order

9783.text: This Rule applies where an individual who is acting as an office-holder (“the outgoing office-holder”):

9785.text: and it is expedient to transfer some or all of the cases in which the outgoing office-holder holds office to one or more office-holders (“the replacement office-holder”) in a single transaction.

9787.text: In a case to which this Rule applies the Court has the power to make an order, (“a block transfer order”), appointing a replacement office-holder in the place of the outgoing office-holder to be:

9788.text: (1) liquidator or provisional liquidator in any winding up; (2) receiver or administrative receiver in any receivership; or (3) supervisor of a voluntary arrangement under Article 13 of the Law.

9790.text: The replacement office-holder must be qualified to act as an insolvency practitioner.

9791.text: Application For A Block Transfer Order

9793.text: An application for a block transfer order may be made to the Registrar for:

9794.text:
(1) the removal of the outgoing office-holder by the exercise of any of the powers in Rule 54.231; (2) the appointment of a replacement office-holder by the exercise of any of the powers in Rule 54.232; and/or   
(3) such other order or direction as may be necessary or expedient in connection with any of the matters referred to above.

9796.text: The powers referred to in Rule 54.230(1) are those in:

9797.text: (1) Articles 46 and 66 (winding up); (2) Article 20 (receivership); and (3) Article 13(6) of the Law (voluntary arrangement).

9799.text: The powers referred to in Rule 54.230(2) are those in:

9800.text: (1) Articles 33, 59 and 71 (winding up); (2) Article 14 (receivership); and (3) Article 13(4) and (5) of the Law (voluntary arrangement).

9802.text: The application may be made by any of the following:

9805.text: An applicant (other than the Registrar of Companies) must deliver notice of the application to the Registrar of Companies at least 5 business days before the hearing of the application.

9807.text: The following must be made a respondent to the application and served by the applicant with it:

9808.text: (1) the outgoing office-holder (if not the applicant or deceased); (2) every person who holds office jointly with the outgoing office-holder; and (3) such other person as the Registrar or Judge directs.

9810.text: The application must contain a schedule setting out:

9813.text: The application must be supported by evidence:

9814.text: (1) setting out the circumstances as a result of which it was expedient to appoint a replacement officeholder; and

9815.text: (2) exhibiting the written consent to act of each person who is proposed to be appointed as replacement office-holder.

9816.text: Action Following Application For A Block Transfer Order

9818.text: The Registrar or Judge may in the first instance consider the application without a hearing and make such order as the Registrar or Judge thinks just.

9820.text: In the first instance, the Registrar or Judge may do any of the following:

9821.text: (1) if the documents are considered to be in order and that the matter is considered straightforward, make an order on the substantive application;

9822.text: (2) give any directions which are considered to be necessary including (if appropriate) directions for the joinder of any additional respondents or requiring the service of the application on any person or requiring additional evidence to be provided; or

9823.text: (3) if an order is not made on the substantive application, give directions for the further consideration of the substantive application by the Registrar or Judge .

9825.text: In deciding to what extent (if any) the costs of making an application under this Rule should be paid as an expense of the insolvency proceedings to which the application relates, the factors to which the Court must have regard include:

9826.text: (1) the reasons for the making of the application; (2) the number of cases to which the application relates; (3) the value of assets comprised in those cases; and (4) the nature and extent of the costs involved.

9828.text: Any appointment made under this Rule must be notified:

9829.text: (1) to the Registrar of Companies as soon as reasonably practicable; (2) to the creditors; and (3) to such other persons as the Court may direct, in such manner as the Court may direct.
